    Home › Types › Freighters & outsize cargo

    Freighters & outsize cargo

    The aircraft built to swallow what nothing else can carry.

    Aircraft here6
    Longest84.00 m
    Combined built1,099
    First flights1965–2018
    What it means

    The category, explained

    Cargo aircraft are shaped by their doors. A nose that hinges upward, a ramp at the back, a fuselage that is essentially a tube of empty volume — every design decision follows from the need to get enormous objects in and out quickly.

    This is where aviation's true giants live. Outsize freighters like the An-124 and the An-225 exist to carry generators, turbines, satellites and locomotives — payloads for which there is simply no alternative. The Beluga and Dreamlifter are stranger still: aircraft built to carry the pieces of other aircraft.

    The aircraft

    Every freighters & outsize cargo here

    Silhouettes are sized by real wingspan — bigger aircraft draw bigger.

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
